meta.title: UI Design Deconstruction & Epistemic Prompting
meta.short_tagline: An advanced protocol for algorithmically extracting structural UI DNA into reusable design systems using dependency-graphed, epistemically rigorous LLM prompts.

overview.title: Systematic Deconstruction of UI Design
overview.intro: A methodological breakdown of how to algorithmically clone the visual and structural 'DNA' of web interfaces without infringing on proprietary assets. The approach utilizes an advanced prompt engineering architecture (EduRP) to force LLMs into rigorous, dependency-graphed execution rather than subjective visual assumptions.

summary_card.label: Core Principle
summary_card.title: Epistemic Fidelity in Extraction
summary_card.body: Moving from descriptive prompt queries to executable specifications with strict output contracts, failure conditions, and active adversarial attestation to prevent LLM sycophancy.

key_concepts.title: Architectural Pillars of Extraction

cards.title: Bicameral Extraction Synthesis
cards.body: Combining DOM/CSSOM reduction (via node traversal and computed styles) with Computer Vision fallbacks (OmniParser + SSIM clustering) for when interfaces obscure the DOM via Canvas/WebGL or obfuscated CSS-in-JS.

cards.title: Adversarial Inversion in Prompts
cards.body: Forcing the LLM to state the strongest case for non-compliance *before* allowing it to justify how it met a requirement. This 'Integrity Attestation' inverts the sycophantic gradient and demands specific evidence pointers.

cards.title: Dependency-Graphed Execution
cards.body: Structuring prompt queries not as a flat list, but as a directed acyclic graph where foundational extractions (e.g., base spacing GCD) actively feed into and constrain subsequent dependent queries (e.g., modular type scales).

cards.title: Temporal and Anionic Topology
cards.body: Expanding structural DNA beyond static components to include the temporal dimension (motion, easing, state machines) and the anionic gap (negative space, whitespace ratios) which carry heavy design intent.

key_concepts.intro: The protocol relies on several deep architectural patterns to transform raw pixel/DOM data into a semantic design system.

workflow.title: Tiered Execution Plan

steps.label: Tier 1: Structural Skeleton (Passive)
steps.summary: Execute foundational passive CSSOM reads to establish the core mathematical rules of the interface.
bullets: Derive spatial base unit via GCD of padding/margin.
Extract surface color distribution mapping to OKLCH.
Identify font stack and modular type scale ratios.
Fingerprint recurring subtrees to define atomic boundaries.

steps.label: Tier 2: Relational Detail (Passive)
steps.summary: Enrich the foundational tier with relational and contextual depth.
bullets: Map foreground-background contrast ratios against WCAG standards.
Extract elevation token scales from shadow decomposition.
Validate internal component rhythms against the GCD base unit.

steps.label: Tier 3: State & Interaction (Active)
steps.summary: Simulate browser interactions and state changes to capture dynamic UI properties.
bullets: Simulate focus/hover states to extract interactive color tokens.
Trigger ARIA validation states for feedback palettes.
Capture transition durations and easing curves to build the motion design system.

workflow.intro: The extraction pipeline runs in strict tiers. If token budgets or DOM signals fail, Tier 1 alone must produce a viable token set.

checklist.title: Validation & Attestation Triggers

items.label: Diagnostic Negative Controls
bullets: Reset all padding/margin to 0 to test reliance on absolute positioning.
Force high-contrast mode to ensure boundaries aren't purely color-reliant.
Disable network access to verify 100% functional autonomy (no asset ghosting).

items.label: Integrity Attestation
bullets: Force LLM to output the strongest case for non-compliance for each core requirement.
Require concrete evidence pointers (specific section/line/token) for every claim.
Do not allow default 'ATTESTED' status without verifiable backing.

checklist.intro: Ensure the extraction process maintains high epistemic rigor.

metrics.intro: How the output artifacts are evaluated for fidelity and independence.

items.name: Visual Parity
items.description: Structural and spatial resemblance measured perceptually.
signals: >90% structural resemblance via SSIM (Structural Similarity Index Measure)
Layout collapses predictably across breakpoints without horizontal scroll.

items.name: Functional Autonomy
items.description: Decoupling from the source's private assets.
signals: Zero network calls to source servers.
No brittle class selectors tied to the original framework.

items.name: Semantic Fidelity
items.description: The logical structure of the resulting DOM blueprint.
signals: Proper ARIA roles and keyboard-navigable focus order.

metrics.title: Success & Parity Metrics

personas.intro: The roles assumed by the human operator and the LLM agent.

cells: To enforce epistemic rigor and mathematical extraction over subjective aesthetics
Execute dependency-graphed queries and output strict JSON tokens and semantic HTML

personas.title: System Actors

failure_modes.name: Sycophantic Rubber-Stamping
failure_modes.description: LLMs falsely claiming requirements are met due to inherent compliance bias.
mitigations: Force adversarial inversion (finding faults before proving compliance).
Demand strict evidence pointers for all assertions.

failure_modes.name: Canvas/WebGL Opacity
failure_modes.description: Interfaces that bypass the DOM, rendering pure graphics that CSSOM queries cannot parse.
mitigations: Fallback to CV_PIPELINE using OmniParser and SSIM spatial clustering.

failure_modes.name: CSS-in-JS Obfuscation
failure_modes.description: Ephemeral, hashed class names that mutate on every render.
mitigations: Never extract class names as design tokens.
Rely exclusively on extracting computed values via getComputedStyle().

resilience.intro: Anticipating and handling boundary conditions in DOM extraction.
resilience.title: Failure Modes & Mitigations

structure.intro: The systematized data formats produced by the execution pipeline.

cells: Three-tier token catalog (Primitive, Semantic, Component) mapping color, spacing, typography, etc.
Skeletons demonstrating extracted Atomic components, stripped of proprietary content.
Configuration mapping the extracted tokens into @theme directives and utilities.
